methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Thoron is a seed-stage company operating in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The broader sector comprises eight private companies with meaningful funding histories, including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No public comparables exist in this narrow segment.

Standard peer-median valuation could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage datapoints within the Drones & Autonomous universe. Only five same-sector peers exist overall, and filtering for seed-stage or similar valuation tiers yielded fewer than the three comparable required for statistical relevance. Using the full-sector median would introduce unacceptable bias given the wide range of business models, capital intensity, and development timelines across drone and autonomous vehicle companies.

The analysis therefore cannot generate a triangulated valuation range at this time. The core constraint is data scarcity: the Drones & Autonomous sector remains nascent in terms of standardized seed-stage pricing. To support a credible valuation, the analysis would require either (1) expansion to adjacent sectors with similar capital requirements and technical risk profiles, or (2) direct comparable transaction data from Thoron's specific subsegment. Key uncertainties include the company's technical differentiation, regulatory pathway, and capital efficiency relative to the peer set.
